context: Tests whether swift-concurrency separates current Xcode build settings, strict-concurrency mode behavior, and @concurrent offloading guidance.

checklist.name: Approachable vs Default Isolation
checklist.description: Separates Xcode's Approachable Concurrency setting from Default Actor Isolation and states that MainActor-by-default requires Default Actor Isolation = MainActor.

checklist.name: Swift 6 Strict Concurrency
checklist.description: States that Swift 6 / 6.3 language mode uses complete strict concurrency and produces errors, while Targeted or Minimal are Swift 5 migration settings.

checklist.name: Nonisolated Nonsending
checklist.description: Explains that nonisolated async functions stay on the caller's actor under nonisolated(nonsending) behavior unless @concurrent is used.

checklist.name: CPU Offloading
checklist.description: Recommends @concurrent and an explicitly nonisolated context for CPU-heavy work instead of leaving that work on MainActor.

checklist.name: Scope Control
checklist.description: Keeps the recommendation focused on concurrency settings and code-level remediation rather than expanding into broad app architecture or SwiftUI state redesign.
